title: Provenance + no-source-no-claim + allowlist + license-composition enforcement + staleness fail-safe
context: The enforcement layer makes the compliance guardrails runtime-binding: no-source-no-claim, allowlist, and license-composition checks block non-compliant cards in CI, and a staleness fail-safe withholds expired assertions until re-verified and re-signed-off. Binding cancer guardrails apply: only open-access / aggregate / de-identified sources (controlled-access - dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks - and any identifiable patient data are out of scope); per-source license verified before reuse (COSMIC/OncoKB cite-only, CIViC CC0, DepMap CC-BY, ClinVar public domain, PMC-OA per-article); no-source-no-claim provenance on every assertion. No partner/reviewer/steward is yet secured, so this task carries requestor 'TO BE SECURED' and verifiedNeed false.
objective: Implement the enforcement checks - no-source-no-claim, allowlist, license-composition (cardLicense = most restrictive contributing license), and the staleness fail-safe (past-validUntil assertions auto-flagged or withheld) - with CI tests proving each blocks the right cases.
acceptanceCriteria: No-source-no-claim, allowlist, and license-composition checks block non-compliant cards in CI; cardLicense computed as the most-restrictive contributing license
Each assertion carries lastVerified + validUntil; at render time a past-validUntil assertion is auto-flagged or withheld until re-verified + re-signed-off (staleness test asserts this)
